title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs abnf, tokens, whitespace, line endings, limits, and parser architecture from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the strict bounded incremental parser architecture. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for strict bounded incremental parser architecture after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
Require CRLF at HTTP/1.1 structural boundaries and document any local robustness exception without parser disagreement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Abnf Derivation
steps.instruction:
Order the Abnf Derivation evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Abnf Derivation.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Abnf Derivation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Abnf Derivation, keep Abnf Derivation connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A review protocol team handles case 6225 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate strict grammar and resource limits before any application effect; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Abnf Derivation has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
